claim: OpenAI CFO explored value sharing model for drug discovery: firms give OpenAI percentage of discovered drugs in exchange for compute access.
actionable: Understand value-sharing licensing model; negotiate cap on IP percentage if using frontier model compute for high-value research.

claim: Value sharing is specific to high-cost drug discovery; NOT applicable to general Chat GPT usage or coding.
actionable: Clarify scope with OpenAI if negotiating compute partnerships; ensure value sharing applies only to specific high-value domains.

claim: Anthropic and Google DeepMind also exploring data licensing and IP partnerships with biotech startups for drug discovery.
actionable: Compare licensing terms across frontier labs; consolidate compute negotiations with single provider for better terms.

claim: Stanford University claims ownership of all inventions conceived by students/researchers on campus or using university resources, even on personal time.
actionable: Review IP ownership clauses in university agreements before conducting research involving AI; negotiate exceptions if needed.

claim: 23andMe and GSK partnership for genetic data: 23andMe failed to monetize and went bankrupt March 2025; data partnerships may create false revenue expectations.
actionable: Avoid overvaluing IP partnerships without sustainable revenue model; ensure core business remains profitable.

claim: If AGI is reached, frontier model owners will consolidate compute-driven economics; smaller firms risk permanent economic stagnation without access.
actionable: Begin negotiating long-term compute partnerships now; plan for scenario where model capabilities are monopolized.
